content: What is the easiest way to run doctests locally? I thought the bots may pick them up but maybe not always, this bit doesn't seem correct https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/3857af38e57a80b15b994e19d1f4301cac796481/rust/macros/lib.rs#L308-L312 (at import the module is called kernel::macro and within the macro it's kernel::macros
You mean running as unit tests? That block you referred is "ignored".

FWIW, I usually run as unit tests by:

./tools/testing/kunit/kunit.py run --make_options LLVM=1 --arch x86_64 --kconfig_add CONFIG_RUST=y
Wow, I totally missed that all macros are marked ignore. Any clue the reason?
Thanks for the command. Maybe we should put that on the website too
Hm. Seems like even after removing the ignore the macros doctests don't get picked up
Looks like the makefile for testing macros/ is missing ;-)

Boqun Feng said:

Looks like the makefile for testing macros/ is missing ;-)

Ah, no. Since macros doesn't have in-kernel dependency, so kunit is not needed to run the test. You can simply run the doctest for macro with:

make rusttest

;-)
Any clue why the tests are all ignore then?

Trevor Gross said:

Oh, that's awesome!

Because tests have in-kernel dependencies ;-) as I just found out.
Huh. It seems like it would be nice if we could run them somehow, buildbots too, as a sanity check at least
